name: Mockito
description: Most popular mocking framework for Java/Kotlin, providing readable stub/verify syntax and an inline byte-code-manipulating mock-maker for final classes.

seo.title: Mockito — Test Automation / Device Cloud
seo.meta_description: Mockito: Most popular mocking framework for Java/Kotlin, providing readable stub/verify syntax and an inline byte-code-manipulating mock-maker for final classes

when_to_use: Stub and verify Java/Kotlin service dependencies
Inline mock-maker for mocking final classes and methods
Argument captors for inspecting method call parameters
Spy on real objects while stubbing specific methods

pros: De facto standard for JVM mocking
Inline mock-maker handles finals and statics
Massive community, docs and Stack Overflow coverage
cons: Static and final mocking can be footgun-heavy
Kotlin ergonomics less pleasant than MockK
Bytecode manipulation can clash with agents
review.verdict: The obvious default for Java mocking, with the usual caveat that over-mocking hurts design.
review.best_for: Java teams doing any real unit testing on non-trivial codebases
review.not_for: Kotlin-first teams who will be happier with MockK
